features: TL-UL CSR read/write access for 15-register map
watchdog enable, bark, bite, pet, and lock behavior
alert_o assertion on watchdog bite threshold
reset initialization and read-only/write-only register behavior
reserved-bit handling and partial 64-bit register write sequencing

directed_tests.name: reset_and_register_smoke
directed_tests.intent: Verify reset values for all readable CSRs, confirm mtime and watchdog count start at zero, and validate TL-UL read/write access to the full register map.

directed_tests.name: mtime_prescaler_basic
directed_tests.intent: Program prescaler values 0, 1, and a mid-range value to confirm mtime increments at the expected rate and remains monotonic.

directed_tests.name: timer0_compare_interrupt
directed_tests.intent: Program mtimecmp0 below and above current mtime, enable intr_o[0], and verify interrupt assertion, W1C clearing, and re-assertion behavior.

directed_tests.name: timer1_compare_interrupt
directed_tests.intent: Repeat comparator validation for mtimecmp1, including INTR_TEST injection and INTR_STATE clearing.

directed_tests.name: 64bit_compare_partial_write
directed_tests.intent: Write comparator LOW and HIGH halves in both orders to validate intermediate compare states and ensure no atomic-write assumption is required.

directed_tests.name: watchdog_enable_pet_bark_bite
directed_tests.intent: Enable watchdog, set bark and bite thresholds, observe watchdog_count progression, verify bark interrupt, pet reset, and bite alert assertion.

directed_tests.name: watchdog_lock_behavior
directed_tests.intent: Set wd_lock, attempt to clear or modify watchdog control fields, confirm lock persistence until reset, and verify WATCHDOG_PET remains writable.

directed_tests.name: interrupt_masking_and_test
directed_tests.intent: Exercise INTR_ENABLE masking, INTR_TEST self-triggering, and confirm output pins only assert when both state and enable are set.

directed_tests.name: reserved_bits_and_ro_wo_access
directed_tests.intent: Write reserved bits in PRESCALER and WATCHDOG_CTRL, attempt writes to RO registers, and confirm readback/side-effect behavior is compliant.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzz_sanity
random_tests.intent: Run constrained-random TL-UL reads/writes across the 64-byte window with scoreboard checking for legal CSR behavior, focusing on accessible registers and expected read-only/write-only responses.

random_tests.name: timer_watchdog_constrained_random
random_tests.intent: Randomize prescaler, comparator thresholds, watchdog thresholds, enable/mask settings, and pet timing to stress concurrent timer and watchdog interactions.

random_tests.name: interrupt_state_random_sequence
random_tests.intent: Randomly toggle INTR_ENABLE, inject INTR_TEST, and clear INTR_STATE while checking that interrupt outputs track the logical AND of state and enable.

risk_areas.reason: Lock semantics are security-sensitive and easy to implement incorrectly; must ensure wd_lock cannot be cleared except by reset while WATCHDOG_PET remains functional.
Comparator registers are split across two 32-bit writes and intermediate states can cause premature or missed interrupts if ordering is mishandled.
Timer increment rate depends on (prescaler + 1) cycles; off-by-one errors are common and directly affect mtime and compare timing.
Bark should warn before bite; incorrect threshold comparison or counter reset behavior can break escalation flow.
W1C behavior, masking, and software test injection are core CSR behaviors and likely to expose register decode or side-effect bugs.
RO/WO access violations and reset value mismatches are common integration issues and can be checked quickly in a short budget.
Upper bits in PRESCALER and WATCHDOG_CTRL must read as zero; improper masking can indicate decode or storage bugs.
